description: Design two solar array configurations (0° flush and 15° tilted with spacing) on a 50x50m flat roof in Seattle, WA. First, set the location to Seattle and draw a 50x50m flat foundation or building roof. Scenario A (Flush Mount): Fill the 50x50m area with a dense array of solar panels set to a 0-degree tilt (no row spacing). Run the 'Annual Yield Analysis' from the Analysis menu, save the project as '~/Documents/Energy3D/flush_array.ng3', and record the total annual yield (in kWh) into a new file '~/Documents/Energy3D/flush_yield.txt'. Scenario B (Tilted Mount): Modify the array to a 15-degree tilt facing South. Apply sufficient inter-row spacing so the panels do not shade each other during winter. Run the 'Annual Yield Analysis' again, save the project as '~/Documents/Energy3D/tilted_array.ng3', and record the new total annual yield into '~/Documents/Energy3D/tilted_yield.txt'. Finally, write a recommendation into '~/Documents/Energy3D/recommendation.txt' stating which array configuration yielded the highest total annual energy for the given roof area.
